lines: A tin ledger hangs at the stair-head on a nail, the warden's hand keeping count of the dead the way it kept count of everything else.

'Quarantine House Seventeen, the under-galleries. I laid them down by the cart papers because names made the orders harder to obey. The stair divided sickness from dissent; the lower cells took the condemned, then anyone whose voice carried. A tidy plan, until I needed to find the people I had been ordered to forget.'
'Four hundred and some before the running total failed me, and still they came up the Hallowfen road faster than the cold could take them. I made room because a house keeps the dead it is given.'
